title: Largest known bottlenose dolphin stranding hits Cape Cod
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Up to 30 bottlenose dolphins stranded off Brewster's coast, the largest mass stranding in the region.
Rescue teams successfully refloated 19 dolphins Tuesday, monitoring their condition with satellite tags.
Increased dolphin strandings are linked to changing tides around Cape Cod's Wellfleet and Eastham areas.

title: Personal ties are enhancing the tall ships experience at Sail Boston
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 festival features nearly 60 visiting vessels, including Canada’s Bluenose II and Bermuda's Spirit of Bermuda.
Ships available for deck tours connect visitors to their heritage, highlighting historic ties between Boston and Maritime provinces.
Festival fosters international friendship and cultural exchange, with schooners drawing tourists and locals seeking historical connections.

title: ‘I can’t believe that just happened’: Noah Kahan celebrated a hole-in-one at Boston Golf Club
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Noah Kahan hit a hole-in-one on hole 11 at Boston Golf Club using a five iron.
Kahan posted a video of the aftermath on social media, expressing disbelief at the shot.
The musician recently achieved four consecutive sellouts at Fenway Park before the golf feat.

title: Boston rents are down again. See how much the average apartment now costs.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's average apartment rent is $2,930, down from $3,054 the previous year, marking a decline over 13 months.
Since 2021, over 37,000 multi-unit buildings have been added, increasing Boston's vacancy rates to 3.2%.
Boston's rental market is impacted by reduced international student enrollment and a softening labor market, affecting demand.

title: Mass. teen injured jumping off 64-foot N.H. waterfall
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old from Massachusetts was injured after jumping off a 64-foot waterfall in Glen Ellis Falls, New Hampshire.
The teen landed on rocks and suffered significant but non-life-threatening injuries, officials reported.
Rescue teams transported the teen to a hospital for treatment after assessing his condition on site.

title: Man allegedly damages ‘several’ cars with wooden stick and cinder block in East Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: A Roxbury man allegedly damaged cars in East Boston using a wooden stick and cinder block.
Police arrested 27-year-old Revanth Kumar Mahesh after responding to the vandalism near 279 Sumner St.
Mahesh is charged with damaging cars and expected to be arraigned in East Boston District Court.

title: Local obituary: Louisa Gag, 36, lifelong Boston resident and safe streets advocate
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Louisa Gag, a 36-year-old Boston resident and safe streets advocate, died on July 9 in a bicycle accident.
Gag improved Boston's livability through roles at Boston Transportation Department and Livable Streets Alliance.
Her educational background includes magna cum laude from University of Rochester and studies at Tufts University.

title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria 'Tori' Amore, 17, was last seen in Waltham on July 3, entering a dark sedan on Lexington Street.
Amore has severe food allergies, asthma, and seizures; she left without an EpiPen or inhaler, police said.
Waltham police urge anyone with information to call 781-314-3600, as they seek public assistance in the search.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth Monday.
The dump truck driver stayed at the scene and was cooperative; no charges or citations have been announced.
The crash is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, was charged with assault after an incident at Burger King in East Boston on June 30.
Donovan allegedly attacked an employee, punched a customer using racial slurs, and resisted arrest at Maverick Station.
Donovan pleaded not guilty at arraignment, was released on recognizance, and is barred from Maverick Square and Burger King.
